artPrompt: The dramatic instant a vast clock-tower mechanism lurches, its single great blank hand creeping halfway past midnight as cold gold sparks and ash rain down over kneeling ashen-robed figures far below. The blood-dark sky cracks with sickly light. The towering blank clock face dominates the upper wide frame, doom dripping downward. Wide 4:3 landscape, bone white, deep black, and cold gold, solemn end-times dread, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Half Past the Apocalypse
text:
Put half a doom counter on the Doomsday Clock. (Yes, half. Round toward doom when anyone asks.)
Prophecy (Predict heads or tails, then flip a coin and catch it but do not look.) Make a Prophecy on a coin you cannot see. If you guessed right, gain 6 life. If wrong, gain 3 life and put the other half of a doom counter on, completing one whole counter and a whole dread.
flavorText: The clock is half past the end. The end is half past caring.
